label: Featured Work
title: 2021 UK Digital-First Census
intro: The UK census runs once every ten years. The 2021 census was the first to be designed as digital-first — a fundamental shift in how the country would count itself, and an infrastructure challenge at a scale few systems ever face.

sections.heading: The challenge
sections.body: The existing survey data collection platform was running on AWS. The architectural team had two mandates: prove that the system was cloud-portable by migrating it to Google Cloud Platform, and validate that it could handle the anticipated peak load — roughly one million households attempting to submit simultaneously on census day.

sections.heading: My role
sections.body: I joined the ONS architecture team as a technical lead, working across software engineering, DevOps and architecture. I led the AWS-to-GCP cloud portability work, introducing Kubernetes-native deployments as part of the migration. I also designed the performance testing strategy — generating a million concurrent, realistic synthetic users is a non-trivial problem in itself, requiring careful modelling of submission behaviour and infrastructure headroom.

sections.heading: The outcome
sections.body: On 21 March 2021, the census launched and the digital collection system held. The groundwork laid during the architecture phase — cloud portability, Kubernetes orchestration, validated performance envelopes — proved robust under real-world load. It remains one of the largest public-sector digital delivery projects in UK government history.
